steps.title: Spot the defining feature of synthetic languages
steps.content: The notes establish that synthetic languages are defined by case systems where nouns change their form based on grammatical function — the key feature that distinguishes them from analytic languages, which use word order and auxiliaries instead.

steps.title: Apply the feature to the choices
steps.content:
C directly explains why Cree is synthetic by identifying its case system and how nouns change forms based on function.

The other choices either describe analytic languages (A), set up a vague contrast (B), or mention noun roles without the mechanism (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
